raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX I ordered some sandals from Bloomingdales online. In the next few days I did not receive a confirmation of my purchase so I assumed the order did not go through for some reason. I NEVER received the shoes. As I pay this CC bill online and had not ordered or bought any other item recently I assumed I had no balance on my card, so did not even look up the monthly statement. I was surprised when I started seeing emails and getting collection calls about my account. Four times I called Bloomingdales and spend a lot of time on hold about this issue. They first said I did not call in time-within 30 days-but would delete the charge. As the weeks went by I was still getting collection calls and emails, so I called 3 more times and each time they said they were investigating the matter and the last time they said they would not delete the charge.

When I order from most other vendors, I most always get a 1. Confirmation of the order. 2. Notice that the items have shipped ; and 3. Notice that the item has been delivered. Bloomingdales did not notify me in anyway about the purchase and was surprised to have them tell me it was delivered by XXXX. Rarely see strangers around my home, ( a townhouse ) and whatever else I have ordered, I have always received it. I have had the CC for 35 years or more and never had an issue. My credit has been damaged and feel this is not a proper resolution. Please assist me. Thank you, XXXX
